mission.title: Install and Configure Qdrant
mission.description: Qdrant is a high-performance vector database designed for AI applications, enabling efficient storage, search, and management of vector data. It is ideal for use cases involving neural networks and semantic-based matching. Key trade-offs include the need for careful resource management and configuration to optimize performance.

steps.title: Install the Qdrant CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Qdrant command-line interface, which is necessary for interacting with the Qdrant service.

```bash

curl -sSL https://github.com/qdrant/qdrant/releases/download/v1.17.0/qdrant-cli-linux-amd64 -o /usr/local/bin/qdrant

chmod +x /usr/local/bin/qdrant

```

> Download the Qdrant CLI binary.
> Make the Qdrant CLI executable.

steps.title: Create a Qdrant cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Qdrant cluster on a managed Kubernetes service with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create qdrant-cluster --num-nodes=3 --zone=us-central1-a --cluster-version=1.30

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created Qdrant cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials qdrant-cluster --zone=us-central1-a

```

> Fetch the credentials for the cluster.

steps.title: Verify the cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks that the nodes in the Qdrant cluster are running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster.

steps.title: Install Qdrant using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs Qdrant on the Kubernetes cluster using Helm, ensuring the correct version is deployed.

```bash

helm repo add qdrant https://qdrant.tech/helm-charts

helm install qdrant qdrant/qdrant --version 1.17.0 --namespace qdrant --create-namespace

```

> Add the Qdrant Helm repository.
> Install Qdrant in the 'qdrant' namespace.

steps.title: Post-installation configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the Qdrant deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment qdrant --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=5 --namespace qdrant

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml

```

> Set up horizontal pod autoscaling.
> Apply monitoring configuration (ensure monitoring-config.yaml is defined).

uninstall.title: Uninstall Qdrant
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Qdrant deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources may remain.

```bash

helm uninstall qdrant --namespace qdrant

gcloud container clusters delete qdrant-cluster --zone=us-central1-a --quiet

```

> Uninstall the Qdrant Helm release.
> Delete the GKE cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Qdrant
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and verify the current state of your deployment. After the upgrade, verify that the deployment is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl cp qdrant/qdrant:/var/lib/qdrant/storage ./backup-storage

helm upgrade qdrant qdrant/qdrant --version 1.17.0 --namespace qdrant

kubectl get pods --namespace qdrant

```

> Backup the current storage.
> Upgrade to the new version of Qdrant.
> Verify that all pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Qdrant'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Qdrant service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Qdrant pods with 'kubectl get pods --namespace qdrant' and review logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace qdrant'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to schedule pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources available to schedule the Qdrant pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up the cluster or reduce the resource requests in the Qdrant Helm chart values.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Qdrant service may not be exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is correctly defined and check the service type (ClusterIP, NodePort, LoadBalancer).

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Qdrant pod may be crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs for errors and adjust the configuration or resource limits as necessary.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced improved indexing algorithms and enhanced API performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old snapshot storage method in favor of a new optimized approach.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.12 or v1.13, ensure to migrate your snapshot storage configuration.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' with all Qdrant pods in the Running state when executing 'kubectl get pods --namespace qdrant'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and cloud CLI installed, and your Kubernetes cluster meets the resource requirements.
